summary: Sprint 1C Identity Runtime Closure — backend-only testing. All 18 pytest cases passed (100%). Verified login/register/me/switch-account endpoints plus bizdomain smoke endpoints. JWT claims (accountId, kind, caps) correctly issued; activeAccount.id != user.id (real accounts, not legacy shim); info-leak safe switch-account (same 403 body for foreign vs non-existent accountId); V1 require_capability correctly delegates to require_capability_v2 as async dep with same signature.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py /me and /switch-account duplicate the Bearer-token decode logic already available in identity_runtime.decode_and_resolve — consider unifying to avoid drift.
/register: referral block swallows all exceptions into {ok:false} — fine, but log stack trace for observability.
Expected note: 'Redis unavailable' WARN spam is harmless (graceful NO-OP fallback), as flagged in the request.
seed_data_creation: Test users prefixed with TEST_<uuid>@test.com are created by register tests (inspector, customer, dup, foreign). They persist in the DB — cleanup not implemented since each run uses unique UUIDs and emails are prefixed for identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base URL comes from frontend/.env EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (https://full-stack-deploy-77.preview.emergentagent.com). Admin creds in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_sprint1c_identity_runtime.py as regression suite for identity-runtime. Migration already ran: 3 accounts (admin, customer, inspector) + 1 account_capability (inspect). Redis WARN spam expected and harmless.
